name: Basecamp API
what_it_does: Basecamp's REST API for managing projects, to-dos, messages, documents, and team communication in Basecamp 3 workspaces.
use_cases: Creating and updating to-do lists and tasks from agent workflows
Posting messages to campfire (chat) or message boards programmatically
Reading project status and due date information for reporting agents
Creating new projects and assigning team members via API
Monitoring project activity and generating progress summaries
not_for: Complex project management with dependencies or Gantt charts
High-frequency operations (Basecamp API has aggressive rate limits)
best_when: Your team uses Basecamp and you need agents to automate task management, notifications, or project status reporting.
avoid_when: You need complex project dependencies, resource management, or you're not a Basecamp customer.

auth.notes: OAuth2 only — no API key option. Must implement OAuth2 flow for all integrations. User agent header is required (Basecamp rejects generic user agents). Account ID required in all API URLs.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 30-day free trial; no permanent free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Basecamp $15/user/month or $299/month flat fee unlimited users
pricing.notes: API access requires a Basecamp subscription. The flat-fee $299/month plan is attractive for large teams. No free tier.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP status codes with occasional JSON body. Error messages are minimal. Validation errors sometimes only indicate which field failed without detail.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Creating a to-do twice creates two items. Agents must check existing items before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth2 is mandatory — no API key option makes server-to-server automation more complex
Account ID must be included in every API URL — fetch from /authorization endpoint first
User-Agent header must be custom (not a generic library default) or requests are rejected
Pagination uses Link header with next URL — not query parameter based
Rate limit of 50 requests/10 seconds is strict for agents processing many projects
Basecamp 2 and Basecamp 3 have entirely different APIs — confirm which version you're integrating with

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 for apps, personal access tokens for personal use. SOC2 Type II. Project and message data may be sensitive business information. Rate limit: 50 requests/10 seconds.
